title: #125 -SpaceX blir största börsnoteringen någonsin - det här behöver du veta!
summary_md:
# #125 -SpaceX blir största börsnoteringen någonsin - det här behöver du veta!

**Feed**: montrosepodden  
**Published**: 2026-06-11  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode opens on a tech-led pullback ('techfrossa') with Nasdaq down >4% in one session, attributing the entire US rally since February to AI/tech names while breadth stayed flat; hosts stress rising US index concentration risk. The bulk of the show covers Apple's incremental AI/Siri push at WWDC (a great company but not necessarily a cheap stock), a Volvo EX60 test drive plus Chinese EV/export competition, and the headline SpaceX IPO — pitched as the largest listing ever at a ~$1.75T valuation with a fixed $135 price, 30% retail allocation but an unusually tiny ~4% free float. Tone is mixed: structural enthusiasm for space/AI tempered by valuation, concentration and FOMO concerns.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🔴 VOLV-B — AB Volvo | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

DNB Carnegie sell rec; intensifying Chinese truck/e-truck competition as China exports and energy-cost advantage surge.

#### Reasoning

Hosts cite a DNB Carnegie sell recommendation on AB Volvo driven by Chinese competition, especially in the shift to electric trucks. China's May exports rose 19.4% YoY (+24.3% to EU) and it builds energy capacity ~80% cheaper than the West, pressuring European industrial competitiveness. Possible EU protective tariffs are a wildcard.

#### Sell Triggers

- if EU enacts protective tariffs shielding domestic truck makers
- if Chinese e-truck market share gains stall
- if AB Volvo defends margins in electrification

> *"DNB Kanega har ju en säljrek på AB Volvo. Alltså inte Volvo Cars som du pratar om nu utan AB Volvo. Av anledning framförallt just konkurrensen med kinesiska lastbilar"*

---

### 🟠 SPCX — SpaceX | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 45%

Largest IPO ever but tiny ~4% free float, FOMO setup, fixed $135 price; only Starlink clearly profitable; hosts have 'mixed feelings'.

#### Reasoning

Hosts are intrigued but cautious: SpaceX lists at a fixed $135 (~$1.75T) with 30% retail allocation yet only ~4% free float — far below the 25% norm and 10% Stockholm minimum — a recipe for FOMO-driven volatility. Starlink (61% of revenue, ~39% margin, scalable) is the attractive asset; the rest requires being a visionary. Valuation is not low.

#### Sell Triggers

- if free float remains illiquid and price spikes on FOMO
- if Starlink subscriber growth decelerates
- if launch/regulatory setbacks hit non-Starlink segments

> *"Jag känner lite blandade känslor... det blir ju kanske ett enkelt recept för den här FOMO-drivna kursuppgången som man söker efter"*

---

### 🟡 AAPL — Apple | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 55%

World-class ecosystem/moat and all-time high, finally advancing on AI (Siri/Gemini), but hosts stress great product doesn't make the stock cheap.

#### Reasoning

Hosts are deeply positive on Apple's integrated hardware-software stack, customer lock-in and moat, and view the WWDC AI/Siri step plus potential Gemini default as strengths, with restrained capex leaving optionality. However they explicitly separate company quality from stock value, noting they can't argue the stock is a buy at an all-time high.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI execution lags peers further
- if upgrade cycle weakens as older models keep OTA updates
- if valuation expands without earnings support

> *"Det betyder inte att, i min mening... att aktien är köpvärd för det"*

---

### 🟢 RUSTA — Rusta | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Stable Q4 with sales growth across all segments, more customers, higher average receipt, strong finances, fast store expansion; consumer trade-down tailwind.

#### Reasoning

New CEO reported a stable Q4 with all segments growing sales despite tough comparables, rising customer count and average receipt, improved profitability (favorable mix plus FX), strong balance sheet and continued high expansion pace (8 new stores). Hosts see low-price retailers benefiting as consumers trade down ahead of a small-cap/discretionary recovery.

#### Sell Triggers

- if same-store sales growth reverses
- if expansion pace strains margins
- if FX tailwinds reverse (low-quality earnings component)

> *"Samtliga segment ökade försäljningen trots mycket starka jämförelsetal och både antalet kunder och snittkvittot växte"*

---

### 🟠 VOLCAR-B — Volvo Cars | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Fastest-growing premium BEV brand in Europe, megacasting/cell-to-body and OTA investments, very high EX60 demand; tiny global share leaves room to gain.

#### Reasoning

Hosts are enthusiastic after the EX60 test drive: strong demand, fastest-growing premium BEV in Europe, large completed investments in megacasting and cell-to-body to lift productivity, and over-the-air updates. Small ~0.7% global share gives runway to take share, though OTA on older cars and Chinese competition could weigh on new-car incentives.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Chinese EV competition compresses pricing
- if OTA updates cannibalize new-car demand
- if EX60 demand fails to convert to volume/margin

> *"vilket bilmärke i Europa inom BEV... som växer allra snabbast i premiumsegmentet... Det är Volvo Cars"*

---

### 🟡 AVGO — Broadcom | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Report below expectations on parts amplified market jitters; capacity fully sold out limits near-term upside surprises.

#### Reasoning

Broadcom's report disappointed the market on some segments and reinforced the tech-frossa, though hosts call it no catastrophe. With all capacity sold out there is little 'wiggle room' to materially over-deliver near term, so the read is cautious rather than negative.

#### Sell Triggers

- if guidance keeps missing on key segments
- if AI/semiconductor demand cools further
- if capacity constraints persist

> *"Broadcom kom med en rapport som marknaden var besviken över. Det var ingen katastrofrapport men den var under förväntningarna på vissa delar"*

---

### 🟠 ASTS — AST Space Mobile | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Google's largest listed-portfolio bet; direct-to-smartphone satellite connectivity, a harder tech than Starlink, sold via telecom operators.

#### Reasoning

Hosts frame AST Space Mobile as a high-upside but technically much harder play than Starlink — connecting ordinary smartphones to satellites with no extra hardware, via partnerships with telecom operators. Endorsed indirectly as Google's top listed holding, but framed as speculative versus Starlink's proven economics.

#### Sell Triggers

- if satellite-to-handset technology fails to scale
- if launch partners (Blue Origin/SpaceX) face delays
- if telecom operator adoption is slow

> *"AST Space Mobile som är det största innehavet i Googles noterade portfölj"*

---
